llm_response.resposta_bruta:
```python
from itertools import permutations

def smallest_divisible_concat(nums, k):
    def is_divisible_concat(p):
        concat = int(''.join(map(str, p)))
        return concat % k == 0

    smallest_perm = []
    for p in permutations(nums):
        if is_divisible_concat(p):
            if not smallest_perm or p < smallest_perm:
                smallest_perm = p
    return list(smallest_perm) if smallest_perm else []

solution: smallest_divisible_concat(nums, k)
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n!)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution generates all permutations of the input array and checks each permutation to see if the concatenated decimal representation is divisible by k. It keeps track of the lexicographically smallest permutation that satisfies this condition. If no such permutation exists, it returns an empty list.
